raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.5,"R1.2":1.0,"R1.3":1.0,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"The section culminates in the contribution claim 'strongest unified segmentation model on standard benchmarks' — that framing must cohere with the evidence actually reported, including the Pascal-Context tie and the unsupported COCO-Stuff figure.","R1.2":"Multiple internal consistency tensions exist: (1) 'consistent state-of-the-art performance across all benchmarks' vs. a result that merely matches SOTA on Pascal-Context; (2) the cross-attention module is credited for gains but the ablation is referenced only obliquely; (3) panoptic PQ is presented as corroborating semantic mIoU without a logical bridge explaining why PQ and mIoU should co-validate.","R1.3":"Specific prior-work numbers are cited for three of four benchmarks, but COCO-Stuff 47.9 mIoU has no named comparison baseline, leaving the claim unanchored. The Pascal-Context figure 'matches' rather than exceeds SOTA yet is framed as SOTA achievement — a direct evidence-claim misalignment.","R1.4":"SOTA claims against InternImage-H and ViT-Adapter are load-sensitive: fairness requires equivalent backbone scale, training data, and evaluation protocol; none are stated, making the delta claims potentially misleading."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should I focus on whether the substantive claims (numerical comparisons, internal logical consistency, SOTA attribution) are defensible, or on improving the prose and writing quality of this section?"}

rationale.R1.1: The section culminates in the contribution claim 'strongest unified segmentation model on standard benchmarks' — that framing must cohere with the evidence actually reported, including the Pascal-Context tie and the unsupported COCO-Stuff figure.
rationale.R1.2: Multiple internal consistency tensions exist: (1) 'consistent state-of-the-art performance across all benchmarks' vs. a result that merely matches SOTA on Pascal-Context; (2) the cross-attention module is credited for gains but the ablation is referenced only obliquely; (3) panoptic PQ is presented as corroborating semantic mIoU without a logical bridge explaining why PQ and mIoU should co-validate.
rationale.R1.3: Specific prior-work numbers are cited for three of four benchmarks, but COCO-Stuff 47.9 mIoU has no named comparison baseline, leaving the claim unanchored. The Pascal-Context figure 'matches' rather than exceeds SOTA yet is framed as SOTA achievement — a direct evidence-claim misalignment.
rationale.R1.4: SOTA claims against InternImage-H and ViT-Adapter are load-sensitive: fairness requires equivalent backbone scale, training data, and evaluation protocol; none are stated, making the delta claims potentially misleading.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus on whether the substantive claims (numerical comparisons, internal logical consistency, SOTA attribution) are defensible, or on improving the prose and writing quality of this section?
